items.rationale: Sees games as the leading edge of web3 adoption, with user-owned game objects flipping the player-game relationship; backing AAA studios building on-chain worlds. His single most recent area of action.
items.age_note: Most recent corpus action (2023-03-21); consistent theme since 2021-10-23.

citations.quote: we couldn’t be happier to partner with @CCPGames on the development of their new AAA title
Expect many NFT-based games to launch, developed by founders with backgrounds at top game studios.

items.rationale: Views NFTs as new digital primitives enabling genuine, interoperable user ownership; especially excited about categories web2 underpays like music and creator monetization.
items.age_note: Peak articulation 2021-12 to 2022-03; not deeply restated in 2023 but consistent with games thesis.

citations.quote: We are starting to see different varieties of NFTs emerge. Here are 7 types of NFTs I’m excited about.
In web3, creators can go direct to their audiences by selling things like NFTs, earning 90% or more

items.rationale: Believes token incentives solve the chicken-and-egg bootstrapping problem and self-marketing, enabling grassroots networks (telecom, storage) to challenge incumbents without ad spend.

citations.quote: A great example of this is Helium, which has used token incentives to build a nationwide grassroots telecom service
Web 3 introduces a powerful new tool for bootstrapping networks: token incentives.

items.rationale: Treats composability as the exponential force in software (like compounding in finance), letting components be reused/remixed to accelerate innovation.

citations.quote: One of the most exciting aspects of web3 is the composability of protocols and tokens.

items.rationale: Argues demand for blockchains will perpetually outpace supply, so many competing infrastructure projects are needed to explore the design space; bullish on Ethereum scaling.
items.age_note: Stated 2021-11; Ethereum merge expectation since fulfilled-direction, not restated 2023.

citations.quote: demand for blockchains will consistently outpace supply.
The more blockchain infrastructure projects, the better.

items.rationale: Sees DAOs as novel coordination constructs for online communities with much design space left to explore for breakthrough products.

citations.quote: DAOs are novel web3 constructs that supercharge online communities by providing more sophisticated coordination methods

items.rationale: Expects open protocols storing social graphs in a credibly neutral way to bring 'open' back and unlock a wave of new consumer social apps.
items.age_note: Stated 2021-10; old and not restated, but flagged as a category to watch.

citations.quote: Expect many more experiments with DAOs, social tokens, decentralized social networks, web3 media, and other emerging categories.

items.rationale: Expects web3 earners to keep tokens in DeFi over tradfi banks for better rates/UX, growing the DeFi user base; cites composability in DeFi protocols.

citations.quote: New web3 users who earn tokens through games and NFTs will likely choose to save those tokens in DeFi protocols

items.rationale: Worries US regulators are taking an adversarial rather than constructive stance, penalizing compliant actors; wants clear rules that protect consumers without driving out innovators. His most recent expressed concern.
items.age_note: Most recent statement (2023-03-22); newly prominent vs earlier corpus.

citations.quote: We hope the US will take a more constructive approach to collaborating with innovators while protecting consumers.
@coinbase has invested heavily in being fully compliant with US laws even when it forced them to move slower

items.rationale: Fears the internet calcifies into a few corporate-owned networks (like broadcast TV) that extract via high take rates and leave no room for startups, with entrenched interests fighting new movements.
items.age_note: Stated 2022-01/06; persistent framing, not restated in 2023.

citations.quote: There is a serious risk the internet turns out like, say, broadcast TV with a few dominant corporate-owned services
Entrenched interests aggressively respond to new movements that might someday threaten them.

items.rationale: Calls ad-reliance the 'original sin' of the internet, trapping creators in a usability-monetization death spiral; the problem web3 economics aim to fix.
items.age_note: Stated 2021-11; older framing, consistent but not restated 2023.

citations.quote: Lack of genuine ownership and the reliance on ads was the original sin of the internet.

items.rationale: Acknowledges possible 2008-style downturn and counsels preserving capital and focus, but frames it as financial-cycle noise separable from the product cycle he stays focused on.
items.age_note: Stated 2022-06-27; treated as transient, subordinate to product-cycle conviction.

citations.quote: If we are headed for an economic downturn, there are some tactical lessons from the 2008 era, namely preserving capital

items.rationale: Treats Ethereum as the programmable base layer with room for ~500x user growth from ~0.2% of internet users, and demand structurally outpacing supply.
items.age_note: Stated 2021-11; not restated as a token call in 2023.

citations.quote: Today, Ethereum has roughly 10M monthly users, which is roughly 0.2% of all internet users.

items.rationale: Flagship example of token incentives bootstrapping a 200,000+ node grassroots telecom network challenging incumbents.
items.age_note: Cited 2021-10 and again 2022-03-06; a16z disclosed investor.

citations.quote: A great example of this is Helium, which has used token incentives to build a nationwide grassroots telecom service

items.rationale: Cited as a token-bootstrapped decentralized storage network with ~12x network growth in a year.
items.age_note: Cited only 2021-10-01; a16z disclosed investor; never restated.

citations.quote: Another network that’s been bootstrapped with token incentives is Arweave, a decentralized storage network.

items.rationale: Cited approvingly as a blockchain that has never been hacked, supporting the security/credibility thesis; mentioned in passing rather than as an active pick.
items.age_note: Mentioned 2021-12-11 only; not an active recommendation.

citations.quote: blockchains like Bitcoin and Ethereum have never been hacked.

risk_regime.rationale: Despite the 2022 downturn and a hostile US regulatory backdrop, his most recent actions (leading a $40M web3 games investment, defending Coinbase) and his product-vs-financial-cycle framework keep him structurally bullish: he separates noisy financial cycles from the crypto product cycle he believes anchors the next era. Risk-on long-term, tactically capital-preserving in a downturn.

notes: Corpus spans 2021-10 to 2023-03; the vast majority is 2021-2022 thought-leadership threads, with only sparse 2023 statements (CCP/EVE games investment, CTO promotion, Coinbase/regulation). Recency weighting therefore elevates web3 games (excited) and US regulation (concerned) as his freshest signals, while NFTs/token-bootstrapping/composability/DAOs/DeFi are persisted convictions stated once or twice and not contradicted. No genuine reversals found — views are remarkably consistent over 18 months; older items marked persisted with age notes. Notable absence: no direct mention of the FTX collapse or specific bear-market token damage, so token-level concern is empty. Many cited projects (Helium, Arweave, CCP Games) are disclosed a16z investments, so excitement is entangled with portfolio interest. This is Dixon's individual, heavily thesis-driven view, not a team consensus.
